Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ENJOY TECHNOLOGY, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Enjoy Technology, Inc.'s demurrer to the first and third causes of action in the third amended complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. The first cause of action sufficiently alleges that the decedent suffered from an uncontrollable impulse to support a claim for wrongful death and the third amended complaint does not show superseding cause as a matter of law. (See, e.g., Whooley v. Tamalpais Union High School District (NC CA 2019) 399 F. Supp. 3d 986). Moving defendant concedes that the asserted procedural deficiency for a survival claim has been remedied. While the first cause of action is not a model of clarity, because the parties understand the intended distinction between the first and third causes of action (the first being wrongful death and the third being survival), no useful purpose is achieved by requiring plaintiffs to file a fourth amended complaint.

Order is signed.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Dexterity, Inc., Roger Hau, and Samir Menon's motion for sanctions is denied.

Defendants' main argument is that plaintiffs erroneously maintained Eggers was not a Dexterity employee at the time of his death. At the pleadings stage, plaintiffs' initial estimation of Eggers' employment length does not warrant sanctions. Defendants also argue for sanctions because the complaint is barred by the Workers' Compensation Act ("WCA"). But plaintiffs' arguments against WCA exclusivity were not frivolous to justify imposing sanctions.

Order signed.

Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KATHERINE A. RABAGO, Esq. of The Arns Law Firm (Tel: 415-495-7800) for plaintiffs;
Atty. MELINDA S. RIECHERT, Esq. of Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP (Tel: 650-843-4000) for defendants Dexterity, Inc. and Roger Hau.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS DEXTERITY, INC., AND ROGER HAU'S MOTION TO TRANSFER.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to change venue is denied.

The complaint alleges that defendants' fraud and intentional infliction of emotional distress caused decedent's death. (Complaint, pars. 76, 84.) CCP 395 provides: "If the action is for injury to person or personal property or for death from wrongful act or negligence, the superior court in either the county where the injury occurs or the injury causing death occurs or the county where the defendants, or some of them reside at the commencement of the action, is a proper court for the trial of the action." Since the death occurred in San Francisco, plaintiffs properly venued this case here. The same would be true under CCP 395.5 since the liability (death) arose in San Francisco. (See CCP 395.5 ["A corporation or association may be sued in the county where the contract is made or is to be performed, or where the obligation or liability arises, or the breach occurs; or in the county where the principal place of business of such corporation is situated, subject to the power of the court to change the place of trial as in other cases."].)

Defendants' cases are inapposite. (See Cubic Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 622, 625; Carruth v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 215, 219-220.) In those decisions, the plaintiff was suing purely for emotional distress. Here, defendants' alleged torts allegedly caused a death.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
